calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, Questions 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 And 6; Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Clifford Chanler, Esq. 203-594-9246 Chanler LLC for Plaintiff.
Steven Chen, Esq. 626-782-5017 for Plaintiff.
Cedar Hobbs, Esq. 858-933-5432 Morrison Foerster for Defendant Michaels Stores.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received from Defense.

ARGUED. Mr. Chen does not contest the TR. Mr. Hobbs disagrees with the ruling and awarding of sanctions; cites civil discovery act, failure to meet and confer, filing premature motion, scope of obligation to supplement responses. The Court comments. Mr. Chen responds, presents timeline. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JAY EPPS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, Questions 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 And 6; Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Jay Epp's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One

Taken Under Submission.*** In addition, further responses are due 12/16/25.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order granting Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories, set one, questions 1,2,3,4,5, and 6 and request for monetary sanctions. (see scanned order for details)***

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-01-2025 Set For Trial On Aug-10-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Jun-24-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Clifford Chanler, Esq., of CHANLER, LLC., Tel: (203) 722-4154 for plaintiff
William Tarantino, Esq., of MORRISON & FOERSTER LLP. Tel: 415.268.7000 for Defendants
Court Reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR# 13859, Tel: 714-471-2597 email: justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com

Hearing is held. Defendant contest the substantive findings of the Court's tentative order that the arbitration agreement at issue is "invalid and unenforceable.". The matter argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Michaels Stores, Inc. and Michaels Companies, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay action is denied.

The court assumes without deciding that there is an arbitration agreement between defendants and plaintiff Jay Epps. However, because Mr. Epps seeks public injunctive relief and the arbitration agreement provides that the arbitrator "may award on an individual basis any relief that would be available in court," the arbitration agreement is "invalid and unenforceable" per McGill v. Citibank (2017) 2 Cal. 5th 945, 961 since it purports to waive Mr. Epps' statutory right to seek public injunctive relief. Relying solely on Maynez v. Walmart, Inc. (CD CA 2020) 479F. Supp. 3d 890, 899, defendants argue that the quoted language from the arbitration agreement allows the arbitrator to award public injunctive relief. Defendants fail to acknowledge, and Mr. Epps fails to point out, that on this point Maynez has been rejected by the First District Court of Appeal in Jack v. Ring LLC (2023) 91 Cal. App. 5th 1186, 1204-1208.

Order is submitted.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Court Reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR# 13859, Tel: 714-471-2597 email: justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com, Reported. (302/HEK)
